---
title: "**DNA Barcoding and Forensics_Student Guide**"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
bibliography: ../book.bib
csl: ../resources/ieee-with-url.csl
format:
    html: default
---

This activity explores forensic uses of DNA barcoding and DNA reference datasets. You will work with real data from several forensic investigations in order to identify species based on a small fragment of DNA.

## Part 1

In Part 1, we'll go over some background context for this activity. Then, you'll choose which case file you want to investigate.

### What is DNA fingerprinting?

DNA fingerprinting and forensics

Expand each case file to learn more about how DNA fingerprinting has been used in forensic investigations.

::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
# Case File: Suspected Parrot Smuggling

overview
why would you use forensics for this?
what they use for fingerprinting
original paper/data
:::

::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
# Case file: Fish Market Fraud

overview
why would you use forensics for this?
what they use for fingerprinting
original paper/data
:::

::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
# Case file: Livestock Necropsy/ Accidental Plant Poisoning?
overview
why would you use forensics for this?
what they use for fingerprinting
original paper/data
:::

::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
# Case file: Mushroom Market Fraud
overview
why would you use forensics for this?
what they use for fingerprinting
original paper/data
:::

## Part 2

What are the samples? Are the samples what they are claimed to be? (use BOLD)

## Part 3

Verify sample identity (use GenBank)

## Part 4

parrots: are the eggs endangered?

fish: are the fish endangered?

plants: are the plants toxic?
